rulings.Case Title: JERRY WAYNE KING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.'S Person(S) Most Qualified And Custodian(S) Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel the deposition of defendant Honeywell International, Inc.¿s person most qualified and custodian of records and request for production of documents: hearing required only to discuss the proposed deposition date and issues related to defendant¿s motion for summary adjudication. The motion to compel will be taken off calendar pursuant to counsel¿s correspondence that the issues raised by the motion are moot. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set Three, And Form Interrogatories, Set Three
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals¿ motion to compel further responses to requests for admission, set three, and form interrogatories, set three, is granted. Plaintiffs¿ amended responses served August 8, 2012 are deficient. The time to provide further responses will be discussed at the hearing. Plaintiffs¿ timeliness objection that the motion was not filed within 45 days of service of plaintiffs¿ responses is overruled. The motion pertains to plaintiffs¿ amended responses served August 8, 2012, making the motion timely. Plaintiffs¿ timeliness objection that the motion is being heard within 15 days of the trial date and that discovery is closed is overruled. Defendant obtained a court order allowing this motion to be heard on this date and such order reasonably allows the court to rule on the motion, including a ruling granting the motion and compelling plaintiffs to provide further responses. (C.C.P. 2024.020, 2024.050.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Hill Brothers Chemical Company¿S Notice Of Taking Deposition Of John Conroy After Close Of Discovery; Plaintiffs Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion for protective order regarding Hill Brothers Chemical Company¿s notice of taking deposition of John Conroy after close of discovery is denied as moot pursuant to the court¿s August 16, 2012 order extending the discovery cut-off through August 22, 2012 for the limited purpose of allowing Mr. Conroy¿s deposition to proceed. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
